questions.q: What did Gandhari do upon marrying the blind Dhritarashtra?
options: She bound her eyes with a cloth for life
She vowed never to return to Gandhara
questions.explanation: Refusing to enjoy a sense her husband lacked, Gandhari blindfolded herself for the rest of her life.

questions.q: Gandhari was a princess of which kingdom?
questions.explanation: She came from Gandhara, the far northwestern kingdom whose name survives in modern Kandahar.

questions.q: Who was Gandhari's husband?
questions.explanation: Gandhari was married to Dhritarashtra, the blind king of Hastinapura.

questions.q: How many sons did Gandhari have?
questions.explanation: Gandhari was the mother of the hundred Kaurava sons, all of whom died in the war.

questions.q: Who was Gandhari's brother?
questions.explanation: Her brother was Shakuni, the cunning dice-master who stayed on at the Hastinapura court.

questions.q: Who was Gandhari's eldest son?
questions.explanation: Duryodhana, born to dreadful omens, was the eldest of Gandhari's hundred sons.

questions.q: Whom did Gandhari curse after the Kurukshetra war ended?
questions.explanation: Holding Krishna responsible for the slaughter, Gandhari cursed him and his Yadava clan.

questions.q: Gandhari's hundred sons were known by which collective name?
questions.explanation: Her sons were the Kauravas, the rival branch of the Kuru dynasty that fought the Pandavas.

questions.q: What was the name of Gandhari's only daughter?
questions.explanation: Besides her hundred sons, Gandhari had one daughter, Duhshala.

questions.q: In which war did all one hundred of Gandhari's sons perish?
questions.explanation: All hundred Kauravas were killed in the eighteen-day war at Kurukshetra.

questions.q: How long did Gandhari's extraordinary pregnancy last?
questions.explanation: She carried her burden for roughly two years before striking her womb in despair.

questions.q: What did Gandhari deliver after striking her womb in despair?
questions.explanation: She delivered a single hard mass of flesh, which Vyasa later transformed into her children.

questions.q: Who divided Gandhari's mass of flesh into pieces so it could become her children?
questions.explanation: The sage Vyasa cut the ball of flesh into a hundred and one pieces and ordered them carefully nurtured.

questions.q: In what were the pieces of Gandhari's ball of flesh incubated?
questions.explanation: Each piece was placed in a jar of ghee, from which the Kauravas were born over time.

questions.q: What news drove Gandhari to strike her own womb in frustration?
options: Dhritarashtra threatened to take a second wife
She heard Kunti had already given birth
questions.explanation: Learning that Kunti had borne Yudhishthira while her own pregnancy dragged on, she struck her womb in despair.

questions.q: Pleased by her devoted hospitality, who granted Gandhari the boon of a hundred sons?
questions.explanation: In Vyasa's epic the sage himself grants the boon for her service, though some retellings credit Shiva.

questions.q: What happened when Gandhari finally lifted her blindfold to gaze upon Duryodhana before his last duel?
options: He was blinded by her stored power
Her gaze made his body adamant, but his covered thighs stayed weak
He gained the strength of a thousand elephants
questions.explanation: In this famous episode from popular retellings, her stored power hardened his body like thunder, except the loins he had covered.

questions.q: What curse did Gandhari pronounce upon Krishna after the war?
options: That the Yadavas would destroy one another and he would die ingloriously
That he would be reborn a mortal a hundred times
That Dwarka would burn that very night
That he would never find peace in Vaikuntha
questions.explanation: She cursed Krishna to watch his Yadava kinsmen slaughter each other and to meet an unheroic death himself.

questions.q: When Duryodhana sought his mother's blessing of victory, what would Gandhari say?
options: Where dharma is, there victory is
My gaze will shield you in battle
questions.explanation: Instead of wishing him victory, she answered with the famous words that victory lies where righteousness lies.

questions.q: In the Stri Parva, what did Gandhari do with the divine sight Vyasa granted her?
options: She watched her sons ascend to heaven
She replayed the game of dice
She surveyed the corpse-strewn battlefield and mourned the slain
questions.explanation: Granted divine vision, she described the devastation of Kurukshetra in one of the epic's great laments.

questions.q: Who was Gandhari's father, the king of Gandhara?
questions.explanation: Gandhari and Shakuni were children of King Subala, which is why Shakuni is called Saubala.

questions.q: How many years after the war did Gandhari's curse on the Yadavas come true?
questions.explanation: Thirty-six years after Kurukshetra, the Yadavas destroyed each other in a drunken brawl at Prabhasa.

questions.q: What happened when Gandhari's angry glance fell upon Yudhishthira's feet after the war?
options: His feet were rooted to the ground
questions.explanation: Her gaze, slipping past the blindfold's edge as he bowed, burned Yudhishthira's toenails black with its stored power.

questions.q: The hundred-and-first piece of Gandhari's divided flesh became which child?
questions.explanation: Gandhari wished for a daughter as well, so Vyasa set aside one extra portion, which became Duhshala.

questions.q: Whom did Gandhari's daughter Duhshala marry?
questions.explanation: Duhshala married Jayadratha, the Sindhu king who later sealed the wheel formation against Abhimanyu.

questions.q: What reason did Gandhari give for blaming Krishna for the war?
options: He broke his vow never to fight
Though able to stop the carnage, he let kinsmen destroy each other
He counseled every broken rule on the battlefield
He denied Duryodhana the use of his armies
questions.explanation: She charged that Krishna, fully capable of preventing the slaughter, had looked on with indifference.

questions.q: How did Gandhari's life come to an end?
options: By ritual fasting on the Ganga
In Dwarka as the sea rose
In a forest fire alongside Dhritarashtra and Kunti
questions.explanation: Years after retiring to the forest, she perished with Dhritarashtra and Kunti in a wildfire they did not flee.

questions.q: How did Krishna respond when Gandhari cursed him and his clan?
options: He begged her to retract the curse
He calmly accepted it, saying the Yadavas' end was already ordained
He countered the curse with a blessing
He vowed that he would outlive it
questions.explanation: Krishna accepted the curse serenely, observing that none but the Yadavas themselves could destroy the Yadavas.

questions.q: During the failed peace negotiations before the war, what role did Gandhari play?
options: She was summoned to court and sternly counseled Duryodhana to make peace
She kept silent out of loyalty to her sons
She urged Dhritarashtra to imprison Krishna
She departed for Gandhara in protest
questions.explanation: In the Udyoga Parva, Dhritarashtra had Gandhari brought to the court, where she rebuked Duryodhana and urged peace in vain.

questions.q: Gandhari's lifelong blindfold is traditionally celebrated as an act of what?
options: Penance for the sins of Gandhara
Supreme wifely devotion, sharing her husband's blindness
questions.explanation: The epic frames it as the ultimate pativrata gesture of sharing her husband's condition, though some modern retellings read it as protest.
